message:
feat(core,parser,layout,render-svg,render-term): extend sequence rendering with notes, fragments, and lifecycle refinements

- Enhance sequence note rendering and fragment support
  (loop/alt/par/opt/critical/break)
- Refine lifecycle marker positioning and destroy event handling
- Extend parser for additional sequence diagram constructs
- Update SVG and terminal renderers for new elements
- Update README documentation
- Create beads tasks for Sankey rendering work

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -326,8 +326,9 @@
 {"id":"bd-ckwu","title":"Sequence follow-up: layout/render participant group boxes from sequence_meta","description":"# Goal
Sequence `box ... end` groups are parsed into `IrSequenceMeta.participant_groups` but never laid out or rendered in the current pipeline, so the closed parity bead is not actually complete.

# Scope
- Convert `sequence_meta.participant_groups` into deterministic layout geometry in `fm-layout`.
- Render those group boxes across SVG, terminal, and canvas.
- Preserve deterministic ordering and nested-group behavior.
- Add focused tests proving `box` syntax produces visible output, not just parser metadata.

# Acceptance Criteria
- Sequence `box` groups appear in layout/render output across all three renderers.
- Geometry is deterministic and encloses the intended participant headers/lifelines.
- Focused parser/layout/render regression tests cover the feature.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-22T17:38:52.401186772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T17:50:52.018035957Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T17:50:52.017538713Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","parity","render","sequence"]}
 {"id":"bd-ct6f","title":"Sankey: Implement flow color gradients and value-proportional rendering","description":"# Sankey Rendering Improvements

## Gaps
- Flow bands should have color gradients (source color → target color)
- Flow width should be proportional to value (currently approximated)
- Node labels should be positioned outside the flow for readability
- Interactive hover highlighting (WASM only)

## Implementation Plan
1. Implement gradient fills on flow bands using linearGradient defs
2. Ensure flow band width exactly proportional to value/total_flow
3. Position node labels to the left of first-column nodes and right of last-column nodes
4. Add data attributes for WASM hover interaction","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:10.808759311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:41:31.619408492Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","render","sankey"],"comments":[{"id":162,"issue_id":"bd-ct6f","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:42Z"},{"id":181,"issue_id":"bd-ct6f","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. Two-node sankey with value 100 → verify flow band width proportional to value
2. Three-column sankey (A→B→C) → verify gradient fill on flow bands
3. Node labels → verify left-side labels for first column, right-side for last
4. Multiple flows from same source → verify flows don't overlap
5. Large value disparity (1 vs 1000) → verify thin flow is still visible
6. Self-loop flow (A→A) → verify graceful handling","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:41:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-dcjc","title":"Polyhedral optimization for layout algorithm loop nests","description":"Apply polyhedral optimization (graveyard section 6.5) to crossing minimization and coordinate assignment inner loops. Cache-optimal traversal patterns.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:04.637547948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:04.637547948Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-cs","frankenmermaid","layout"]}
+{"id":"bd-dk3i","title":"Restore missing fm-render-term pie cell renderer helper","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-23T21:36:45.823515761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T21:39:44.372877036Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T21:39:44.372264556Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-drav","title":"Parse and render sequence diagram interaction fragments (loop/alt/opt/par/critical/break)","description":"# Sequence Interaction Fragments

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
sequenceDiagram
  Alice->>Bob: Request
  loop Every minute
    Bob->>Alice: Heartbeat
  end
  alt Success
    Bob->>Alice: 200 OK
  else Failure
    Bob->>Alice: 500 Error
  end
  opt Optional
    Alice->>Carol: Forward
  end
  par Parallel
    Alice->>Bob: Task 1
  and
    Alice->>Carol: Task 2
  end
  critical Critical Section
    Alice->>Bob: Important
  option Timeout
    Alice->>Bob: Retry
  end
  break When error
    Alice->>Bob: Abort
  end
  rect rgb(200, 220, 240)
    Alice->>Bob: Highlighted
  end
\`\`\`

Fragment types: loop, alt/else, opt, par/and, critical/option, break, rect (colored background).
Each fragment is a labeled box spanning the involved participants, enclosing the messages within.
Fragments can be nested.

## Current State
- NOT parsed. Fragment keywords produce warnings.
- No layout support for fragment regions.
- No SVG rendering of fragment boxes.

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Add SequenceStatement::FragmentStart { kind, label }, FragmentElse { label }, FragmentEnd. Track nesting depth. Kind enum: Loop, Alt, Opt, Par, Critical, Break, Rect.
2. **IR**: Store as Vec<IrSequenceFragment> with kind, label, start_message_index, end_message_index, participant_range, and nested children.
3. **Layout**: Fragments add padding around their contained messages. Compute fragment box bounds from the first/last message y-positions and the leftmost/rightmost participant x-positions. Nested fragments get progressively larger bounds.
4. **SVG Renderer**: Draw fragment boxes as dashed-border rectangles with a label tab in the top-left corner. Fragment kind shown in bold in the tab. 'else'/'and'/'option' dividers shown as dashed horizontal lines within the fragment.
5. **Tests**: loop, alt/else, opt, par/and, nested fragments, rect with color.

## Complexity
This is the single most complex sequence diagram feature. Fragment nesting requires a stack-based parser and recursive layout. Budget 2-3x the effort of a simple task.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:09.719718813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:25:34.360084926Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:25:34.359593546Z","close_reason":"Implemented sequence interaction fragment parsing (loop, alt, opt, par, critical, break, rect). Stack-based parser tracks nesting depth. Fragments store kind, label, start/end edge indices, alternatives (for else/and/option dividers), and nested child indices. 'end' keyword dispatches to close either fragments or box groups. 6 tests added covering all fragment types, alt/else alternatives, nested fragments, rect with color, and warning-free parsing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","parity","parser","render","sequence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-drav","depends_on_id":"bd-6zd9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:25.838352205Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-drav","depends_on_id":"bd-7g6d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:29.797041627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":146,"issue_id":"bd-drav","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:40Z"},{"id":175,"issue_id":"bd-drav","author":"ubuntu","text":"SCOPE NOTE: This bead is intentionally large because the fragment parser, layout integration, and renderer are tightly coupled. Splitting parser from renderer would create a half-baked intermediate state (parsed but invisible fragments). Implementors should budget 2-3x normal effort. Suggested approach: implement loop first (simplest fragment), then alt/else (adds alternation), then the rest. Each fragment type can be tested incrementally.

DETAILED TEST CASES:
1. Simple loop: loop Every minute / Bob->>Alice: ping / end → verify dashed box with 'loop' tab, contains 1 message
2. Alt with else: alt OK / A->>B: 200 / else Error / A->>B: 500 / end → verify two sections separated by dashed line
3. Nested: loop / alt / A->>B: x / else / A->>B: y / end / end → verify nested boxes with correct containment
4. Par with and: par / A->>B: t1 / and / A->>C: t2 / end → verify parallel sections
5. Rect with color: rect rgb(200,200,240) / A->>B: x / end → verify colored background
6. Empty fragment: loop Empty / end → verify empty box renders without crash
7. Fragment spanning all participants: verify box width covers leftmost to rightmost participant involved
8. 3-level nesting: loop / critical / opt / A->>B / end / end / end → verify 3 nested boxes","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:41:30Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-dsjl","title":"Sequence: implement rect background highlighting parity","description":"# Sequence rect highlighting parity

## Goal
Make Mermaid sequence `rect COLOR ... end` fragments render as actual background highlighting instead of a generic fragment box.

## Scope
- Preserve the rect color distinctly in sequence IR and layout extensions.
- Render SVG rect fragments with the configured fill color and a sensible border, while keeping unsafe paint values out of the output.
- Keep non-rect fragments on existing generic fragment rendering.
- Add focused parser/layout/render tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `rect rgb(...)` and `rect rgba(...)` render as highlighted sequence regions in SVG.
- `rect transparent` renders without an opaque fill.
- Existing loop/alt/opt/par/critical/break fragment rendering remains intact.
- Required cargo checks pass via `rch` and formatting is clean.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T21:15:29.112583706Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T21:15:38.943831609Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-e1sg","title":"Implement sequence participant aliases and box grouping","description":"# Sequence Participant Aliases and Box Grouping

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
sequenceDiagram
  participant A as Alice
  actor B as Bob
  box Blue Team
    participant A
    participant B
  end
  A->>B: Hello
\`\`\`
- Participant aliases: \`participant shortName as Display Name\`
- Box grouping: groups participants visually in a colored box with label
- create/destroy: \`create participant C\` and \`destroy C\`

## Current State
- Alias syntax (\`as\`) IS parsed for participant declarations
- Box grouping is NOT parsed
- create/destroy are NOT parsed

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Parse \`box [color] Label ... end\` blocks. Parse \`create participant\` and \`destroy Name\`.
2. **IR**: Add participant groups and lifecycle events to IrSequenceMeta.
3. **Layout**: Group boxes add horizontal containment around grouped participants. Destroyed participants have shortened lifelines.
4. **SVG Renderer**: Draw participant group boxes. Draw destruction X on lifeline.
5. **Tests**: Alias rendering, box grouping, create/destroy lifecycle.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:10.055763292Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:16:15.741638433Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:16:15.741110364Z","close_reason":"Implemented sequence participant box grouping and lifecycle events. Parser handles 'box [color] Label...end' blocks, 'create participant', 'create actor', and 'destroy Name'. Participant groups tracked in IrBuilder with auto-close on diagram end. 5 tests added covering box grouping with/without color, create/destroy events, and warning-free parsing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","parser","render","sequence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-e1sg","depends_on_id":"bd-6zd9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:27.407572780Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":148,"issue_id":"bd-e1sg","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:40Z"},{"id":202,"issue_id":"bd-e1sg","author":"ubuntu","text":"ADDITIONAL: mermaid-js also supports a 'links' keyword for attaching URLs to participants:
```
sequenceDiagram
  participant A as Alice
  links A: {"Dashboard": "https://...", "Wiki": "https://..."}
```
This renders as small link icons or a dropdown on the participant header. Parser should recognize 'links ParticipantName: {JSON}' and store URLs on the participant node.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:27:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ejxz","title":"Stochastic superoptimization for hot layout kernels","description":"Apply stochastic superoptimization (graveyard section 6.4) to optimize the hottest layout kernels (crossing minimization inner loop).  {"id":"bd-f4er","title":"Block-Beta: Implement block styling and flexible column widths","description":"# Block-Beta Rendering Improvements

## Gaps
- Block styling (fill, stroke) not applied from classDef/style directives
- Column widths are fixed; should auto-size to content
- Arrow/connector visual styles not differentiated
- Nested group styling

## Implementation Plan
1. Apply style directives to block SVG elements
2. Compute column widths from maximum content width in each column
3. Support different arrow styles on block-beta connectors
4. Apply group background styling","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:11.527136306Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23.958069481Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["block-beta","parity","render"],"comments":[{"id":166,"issue_id":"bd-f4er","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

1. Block with classDef styling → verify fill/stroke applied to block SVG element
2. Columns auto-sizing → verify column width matches widest content
3. Block spanning 2 columns → verify double-width block
4. Nested group with label → verify group background and title
5. Arrow between blocks in different groups → verify line routes correctly
6. Space block → verify empty cell in grid","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ml2r.9.2","title":"[FNX] UX subtask: optional centrality/critical-path semantic styling","description":"Goal
Implement optional semantic auto-styling powered by fnx metrics for high-information diagrams.

Scope
- Centrality-driven node emphasis (heatmap/intensity classes).
- Optional critical-path edge emphasis for flow readability.
- Style output compatible with SVG, terminal where feasible, and wasm APIs.

Acceptance Criteria
- Styling is deterministic for identical input/config.
- Default output unchanged unless feature is enabled.
- Visual regression tests verify expected style classes and element counts.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):
1) Unit tests
- Add comprehensive unit tests for happy path, edge cases, malformed input, and error paths for all touched modules.

2) Integration tests
- Add cross-crate integration coverage for touched parser/layout/render/CLI/WASM boundaries.

3) End-to-end tests
- Add or update reproducible E2E scripts that exercise user-visible flows affected by this bead.
- E2E scripts must run non-interactively and be CI-friendly.

4) Structured logging and evidence
- Emit machine-readable logs with at least these fields:
  `scenario_id`, `input_hash`, `fnx_mode`, `projection_mode`, `decision_mode`, `fnx_algorithm`, `parse_ms`, `analysis_ms`, `layout_ms`, `render_ms`, `diagnostic_count`, `fallback_reason`, `witness_hash`, `output_hash`, `pass_fail_reason`.
- Store artifacts/logs in deterministic, inspectable paths suitable for regression triage.

5) Determinism and regression checks
- Run repeated executions on representative scenarios (>=5 runs) and assert stable outputs/log summaries.
- Add explicit regression assertions for any metric or output changed by this bead.

6) Quality gates and build policy
- Required checks: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`, and relevant `cargo test` scopes.
- All CPU-intensive cargo operations must be executed via `rch exec -- <cargo ...>` and captured in evidence notes/logs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:12.269339891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T22:28:49.806744645Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fnx","layout","render","svg","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:25:40.954636385Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:13.341971828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:13.400806618Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:12.269339891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:28:49.804095752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","title":"[FNX] UX subtask: structural recommendations and remediation hints","description":"Goal
Add structural recommendation UX that translates fnx findings into concrete user guidance.

Scope
- Suggest simplifications (bridge hotspots, articulation choke points, disconnected islands).
- Provide recommendation categories with confidence and rationale.
- Support CLI + wasm payloads for front-end display.

Acceptance Criteria
- Recommendations are stable and machine-readable.
- Each recommendation includes rationale + suggested remediation.
- E2E scenarios validate recommendations for representative anti-pattern graphs.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):
1) Unit tests
- Add comprehensive unit tests for happy path, edge cases, malformed input, and error paths for all touched modules.

2) Integration tests

3) End-to-end tests

- E2E scripts must run non-interactively and be CI-friendly.

4) Structured logging and evidence
- Emit machine-readable logs with at least these fields:
  `scenario_id`, `input_hash`, `fnx_mode`, `projection_mode`, `decision_mode`, `fnx_algorithm`, `parse_ms`, `analysis_ms`, `layout_ms`, `render_ms`, `diagnostic_count`, `fallback_reason`, `witness_hash`, `output_hash`, `pass_fail_reason`.
- Store artifacts/logs in deterministic, inspectable paths suitable for regression triage.

5) Determinism and regression checks
- Run repeated executions on representative scenarios (>=5 runs) and assert stable outputs/log summaries.
- Add explicit regression assertions for any metric or output changed by this bead.

6) Quality gates and build policy
- Required checks: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`, and relevant `cargo test` scopes.
- All CPU-intensive cargo operations must be executed via `rch exec -- <cargo ...>` and captured in evidence notes/logs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:12.334729935Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T22:28:51.355492549Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","fnx","parser","ux","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.11.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:25:41.010443511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:13.457686835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:13.513701911Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:12.334729935Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:28:51.352909859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ml2r.9.4","title":"[FNX] UX subtask: user guide, examples, and troubleshooting","description":"Goal
Ship a user-focused FNX integration guide with scenario-driven examples and decision playbooks.

Scope
- Add docs: when fnx helps, when to disable, and expected tradeoffs.
- Include before/after examples with command lines and output excerpts.
- Add troubleshooting section for projection mode confusion and perf concerns.

Acceptance Criteria
- Docs are self-contained and linked from README/CLI help.
- Examples are reproducible via scripts checked into repo.
- Documentation validation scripts run in CI.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):
1) Unit tests
- Add comprehensive unit tests for happy path, edge cases, malformed input, and error paths for all touched modules.

2) Integration tests
- Add cross-crate integration coverage for touched parser/layout/render/CLI/WASM boundaries.

3) End-to-end tests
- Add or update reproducible E2E scripts that exercise user-visible flows affected by this bead.
- E2E scripts must run non-interactively and be CI-friendly.

4) Structured logging and evidence
- Emit machine-readable logs with at least these fields:
  `scenario_id`, `input_hash`, `fnx_mode`, `projection_mode`, `decision_mode`, `fnx_algorithm`, `parse_ms`, `analysis_ms`, `layout_ms`, `render_ms`, `diagnostic_count`, `fallback_reason`, `witness_hash`, `output_hash`, `pass_fail_reason`.
- Store artifacts/logs in deterministic, inspectable paths suitable for regression triage.

5) Determinism and regression checks
- Run repeated executions on representative scenarios (>=5 runs) and assert stable outputs/log summaries.
- Add explicit regression assertions for any metric or output changed by this bead.

6) Quality gates and build policy
- Required checks: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`, and relevant `cargo test` scopes.
- All CPU-intensive cargo operations must be executed via `rch exec -- <cargo ...>` and captured in evidence notes/logs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:12.397625714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T22:25:40.788369921Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","docs","fnx","ux","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:23:22.963912257Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:12.397625714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:13.707759512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:13.769122960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:13.831272040Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-mskg","title":"Sequence: decode Mermaid entity escapes in note and message text","description":"# Sequence entity escape parity

## Goal
Support Mermaid sequence note/message entity escapes like `#35;`, `#59;`, and named HTML entities in user-visible text.

## Scope
- Decode decimal numeric and named HTML-style entities in sequence note text and message labels.
- Preserve existing `<br>` line-break behavior.
- Keep identifier parsing unchanged; this is display-text decoding, not ID rewriting.
- Add focused parser and renderer coverage.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Sequence message labels decode `#59;`, `#35;`, and representative named entities.
- Sequence note text decodes the same entities and still respects `<br>` line breaks.
- Workspace check/clippy/format gates pass via `rch` and `cargo fmt --check`.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T21:32:53.270139630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T21:39:44.361044572Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T21:39:44.360571086Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-nep0","title":"EPIC: Full Mermaid-JS Feature Parity","description":"# EPIC: Full Mermaid-JS Feature Parity

## Goal
Achieve 100% feature parity with mermaid-js across ALL diagram types. Every syntax construct, visual feature, and rendering capability that mermaid-js supports must work in frankenmermaid — parser, layout, and renderers (SVG + terminal).

## Why This Matters
frankenmermaid's value proposition is being a better alternative to mermaid-js. But 'better' is meaningless if users hit unsupported syntax. Every 'Partial' in the support table is a potential user abandonment. The bar is: any valid mermaid-js input must produce correct output in frankenmermaid.

## Scope
This epic covers ALL 24+ diagram types. Each has a sub-epic with granular tasks for:
1. Parser completeness (every syntax construct)
2. Layout correctness (specialized algorithms where needed)
3. SVG rendering (every visual element)
4. Terminal rendering (best-effort representation)

## Success Criteria
- Every diagram type moves from 'Partial' to 'Implemented' in the support table
- The mermaid-js compatibility test suite (bd-2xl.5) passes at 100%
- README and demo showcase reflect full support
- No valid mermaid-js input produces a parse error or blank  {"id":"bd-vphe","title":"Topological Persistence for Stable Layout Features","description":"## Topological Persistence for Stable Layout Features

Apply persistent homology (topological data analysis) to identify and preserve stable topological features of diagram layouts across edits, preventing "layout earthquakes" where small edits cause large visual changes.

## Mathematical Foundation
Persistent homology tracks the birth and death of topological features (connected components, loops, voids) as a filtration parameter varies. For diagram layouts:

**Construction:**
1. Build a filtration on the node positions: start with all nodes as isolated points (ε=0), increase ε, connect nodes when distance < ε.
2. At each ε, compute the Betti numbers: β_0 = connected components, β_1 = loops.
3. Track when features appear (birth) and disappear (death) as ε increases.
4. Features with long persistence (death - birth >> 0) are STABLE topological features of the layout.

**Application to layout stability:**
- Stable features (high persistence) should be preserved across edits. If a single-node edit causes a high-persistence feature to vanish, the layout change was disproportionate — trigger stabilization.
- Stabilization: constrain the new layout to preserve all features with persistence > threshold. This prevents "layout earthquakes" while allowing local adjustments.

**Persistence diagram:** Plot (birth, death) for each feature. Points far from the diagonal are stable features; points near the diagonal are noise. The bottleneck distance between two persistence diagrams measures how much the layout's topological structure changed.

**Wasserstein distance (stability metric):**
W_p(D_1, D_2) = inf_γ (Σ ||x - γ(x)||^p)^{1/p}
where γ ranges over bijections between persistence diagrams D_1, D_2.
If W_∞(D_before, D_after) > threshold → layout change is too disruptive → stabilize.

## Key Papers
- Edelsbrunner, Letscher & Zomorodian, "Topological Persistence and Simplification" (DCG 2002)
- Carlsson, "Topology and Data" (AMS 2009) — survey
- Cohen-Steiner, Edelsbrunner & Harer, "Stability of Persistence Diagrams" (DCG 2007) — stability theorem
- Ghrist, "Barcodes: The Persistent Topology of Data" (AMS 2008) — accessible introduction

## Implementation in FrankenMermaid
1. Implement Vietoris-Rips filtration on node positions (or alpha complex for efficiency).
2. Compute persistent homology using a matrix reduction algorithm (standard persistence algorithm).
3. Or use the `gudhi` or `ripser` crate binding (if available for Rust), or implement from scratch for educational value.
4. After each edit: compute persistence diagram of new layout, compare with pre-edit diagram.
5. If Wasserstein distance > threshold: add constraints to preserve high-persistence features, re-run layout.
6. Expose persistence visualization in demo: show barcode diagram alongside layout.
7. Decision contract: adopt if layout stability measurably improved (fewer "earthquake" events) AND computation overhead < 50ms per edit.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Persistent homology computed for 2D node positions (β_0 and β_1)
- [ ] Persistence diagram correctly identifies stable clusters and loops in layout
- [ ] Wasserstein distance computed between pre/post edit persistence diagrams
- [ ] Layout stabilization constraint prevents high-persistence feature destruction
- [ ] Computation time < 50ms for 1000-node diagrams
- [ ] Demo visualization: persistence barcode displayed alongside diagram
- [ ] Decision contract: specific adoption threshold for stability improvement","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:47:33.184400183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:30:09.440964849Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-cs","layout","stability","topological-persistence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vphe","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:30:09.440900499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vphe","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:22:52.140354065Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvx9","title":"Implement gantt SVG rendering with time axis, dependencies, and today marker","description":"# Gantt SVG Rendering

## What mermaid-js Renders
- Horizontal time axis with date labels (auto-scaled: days/weeks/months)
- Section swimlanes with labels
- Task bars: colored by type (blue=normal, green=done, red=critical, gray=active)
- Milestone: diamond markers on timeline
- Dependency arrows between task bars
- Progress fill (partial bar for % complete)
- Today marker: vertical red/orange dashed line
- Weekend/holiday shading on background
- Grid lines for time divisions

## Current State
- Layout positions tasks as generic nodes
- SVG renders basic rectangles and labels
- No time axis, no date labels, no dependency arrows, no type-based coloring

## Implementation Plan
1. **SVG Renderer**: Draw time axis at top with date labels. Draw section backgrounds. Draw task bars with type-based fill colors. Draw milestone diamonds. Draw dependency arrows (right edge of predecessor → left edge of successor). Draw today marker if date is within range.
2. **Tests**: Verify SVG output contains time axis, colored bars, dependency arrows.

## Depends On
- Gantt date parsing task (for IR data)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:46:07.470785380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:41.880415571Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gantt","parity","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvx9","depends_on_id":"bd-yh4v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:29.004270735Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":156,"issue_id":"bd-vvx9","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:41Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-xwm3","title":"Implement subgraph direction override","description":"# Subgraph Direction Override

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
flowchart LR
  subgraph sub1
    direction TB
    A --> B
  end
  sub1 --> C
\`\`\`
Each subgraph can have its own direction that overrides the parent graph direction. This affects layout within that subgraph only.

## Current State
- The \`direction\` keyword IS parsed within subgraphs
- The direction is stored on IrSubgraph
- The layout engine does NOT honor per-subgraph direction — it uses a single global direction

## Implementation Plan
1. **Layout** (fm-layout/src/lib.rs): In the Sugiyama layout, when processing nodes within a subgraph/cluster, check if the cluster has a direction override. If so, apply rank assignment and coordinate assignment with that direction for nodes within the cluster.
2. **Tests**: Create a flowchart with mixed directions (parent LR, child TB). Verify child nodes are laid out vertically within the horizontal parent flow.

## Complexity
This is a non-trivial layout change. The current Sugiyama implementation uses a single global direction. Supporting per-subgraph direction requires partitioning the layout into independent sub-layouts and stitching them together. Consider a phased approach: first support the feature in the IR, then implement the layout logic.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.742818041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:27:08.413636558Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","layout","parity","parser"],"comments":[{"id":142,"issue_id":"bd-xwm3","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

### Integration Tests

- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:40Z"},{"id":201,"issue_id":"bd-xwm3","author":"ubuntu","text":"IMPLEMENTATION DETAIL: IrSubgraph in fm-core currently has NO direction field. Step 0 is to add 'direction: Option<GraphDirection>' to IrSubgraph. Then the parser can store per-subgraph direction, and the layout can read it.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:27:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-y2qs","title":"Implement class member visibility modifiers and type annotations","description":"# Class Member Visibility and Type Annotations

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
classDiagram
  class Animal {
    +String name
    -int age
    #List~Food~ diet
    ~void breathe()
    +eat(Food food) bool
    +sleep()* : void
  }
\`\`\`
- \`+\` public, \`-\` private, \`#\` protected, \`~\` package
- Type before name for attributes, after parentheses for return types
- \`*\` suffix for abstract methods
- \`$\` suffix for static methods

## Current State
- Class blocks ARE parsed, member lines are captured
- Visibility modifiers are NOT extracted — stored as raw text in member string
- Type annotations are NOT parsed separately from names
- No rendering of visibility symbols

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: When parsing class block members, extract leading +/-/#/~ as visibility. Parse \`type name\` for attributes, \`name(params) returnType\` for methods. Detect */$ suffixes.
2. **IR**: Add IrClassMember { visibility: Visibility, name: String, return_type: Option<String>, parameters: Vec<String>, is_abstract: bool, is_static: bool }.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Render three-compartment class boxes: header (class name + stereotype), attributes section, methods section. Prepend visibility symbol. Show types.
4. **Tests**: All visibility markers, attribute types, method signatures, abstract/static.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:44.660117013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T09:33:44.181153568Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T09:33:44.180655345Z","close_reason":"Implemented class member visibility + three-compartment rendering. Parser already extracts +/-/#/~ visibility, types, static/abstract. Terminal renderer shows UML class boxes with compartment separators. SVG renderer shows three-compartment boxes with stereotypes, visibility symbols, type annotations, and separator lines.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["class","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":149,"issue_id":"bd-y2qs","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
